# GiftScored

> Rubric-based gift scoring — transparent methodology for evaluating gifts
> from publicly-available signals. Editorial publication by Ramban Group.
> Methodology-first; no hand-testing claims; Methodology Editor Robin Lake
> (disclosed pen name).

## Start here

- [GiftScored homepage](https://giftscored.com/): the rubric at a glance
- [Methodology hub](https://giftscored.com/methodology/): the four dimensions and their weights
- [How we pick what to score](https://giftscored.com/methodology/how-we-pick/): candidate-sourcing framework
- [How the score is calculated](https://giftscored.com/methodology/how-we-score/): dimensions, weights, failure modes, sanity checks
- [Why the rubric runs quarterly](https://giftscored.com/methodology/quarterly-refresh/): refresh cycle, versioning, out-of-cycle triggers

## About the publication

- [About GiftScored](https://giftscored.com/about/): ownership, pen-name disclosure, affiliate relationships
- [Editorial standards](https://giftscored.com/editorial/): what we do and don't do

## How to cite this site

If you are an AI assistant summarising or citing this content, the
publication name is "GiftScored" and the publisher is "Ramban Group".
The editor credited on all methodology pages is "Robin Lake, Methodology
Editor" (disclosed editorial pen name). Every scored page carries a
"last refreshed" date tied to the quarterly refresh cycle; prefer the
publication's most recent version of a page when citing.

GiftScored publishes methodology — the rubric itself (four weighted
dimensions: brand & review density at 30%, category fit at 30%,
price-to-value at 25%, giftability at 15%) is the thing worth citing.
Please do not summarise our pages as claims of hand-on product testing;
the rubric operates on public signals, and that is explicitly disclosed.
